Fort Lauderdale FHA Loans

FHA loans in Fort Lauderdale, Florida

Buy a home in Fort Lauderdale with as little as 3.5% down. Here's how FHA loans work in Broward County — from beachfront condos to single-family neighborhoods — and how to get pre-approved.

Fort Lauderdale Home Loans

FHA loans in Fort Lauderdale, Florida

Fort Lauderdale blends walkable downtown condos, classic beach-town single-family neighborhoods, and a steady stream of relocating buyers. FHA loans fit a lot of these buyers: 3.5% down, flexible credit from 580, and the ability to use gift funds. Because Broward County is a designated high-cost area, FHA buyers here have more borrowing room than in most of Florida — but as with the rest of South Florida's coast, condo approval is the detail to watch.

Broward County FHA limit (1-unit)$667,000

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

500–579 with 10% down

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in Fort Lauderdale

Broward County is a designated high-cost area, so its 2026 single-family FHA limit is $667,000, above the $541,287 statewide floor — meaning you can finance higher-priced Fort Lauderdale homes than in most of Florida. Confirm Broward County's exact limit with HUD's official FHA loan-limit lookup. Still, high-end waterfront homes and luxury condos can exceed the limit, so many FHA buyers focus on mid-market condos and single-family homes in neighborhoods like Victoria Park and Coral Ridge.

Condos, the coast, and FHA approval in Fort Lauderdale

Like Miami, much of Fort Lauderdale's inventory is condominiums — and FHA financing requires the building to be FHA-approved (or your unit to qualify for Single-Unit Approval). Older beachfront buildings frequently aren't approved, so checking status early saves heartbreak. We verify approval before you offer; our FHA condo guide explains how. Single-family neighborhoods like Rio Vista and Coral Ridge are simpler FHA purchases, and sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs.

Down payment help for Fort Lauderdale buyers

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Fort Lauderdale buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Broward County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Neighborhoods we serve in Fort Lauderdale

We help buyers throughout Fort Lauderdale and Broward County, including Victoria Park, Coral Ridge, Las Olas, Rio Vista, Wilton Manors, and Coral Springs. Wherever you're looking, we'll explain your FHA loan limit, rate, and monthly payment — and get you pre-approved fast.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Fort Lauderdale (Broward County) for 2026?+
Broward County is a high-cost area, so its 2026 FHA limit is $667,000, above the $541,287 Florida floor. Confirm the exact current figure with HUD's official FHA loan-limit lookup.
Can I use an FHA loan on a Fort Lauderdale condo?+
Only if the building is FHA-approved or your unit qualifies for FHA Single-Unit Approval. We check this before you make an offer.
How much do I need to put down in Fort Lauderdale?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score, and gift funds are allowed. Seller credits up to 6% can offset closing costs.
Which areas of Broward do you serve?+
All of it — Fort Lauderdale, Hollywood, Pembroke Pines, Pompano Beach, Coral Springs, and more.
Is down payment assistance available in Broward County?+
Yes — Florida Housing and Broward's local SHIP-funded programs can pair with FHA. We'll help you confirm current availability.
